body.title: [sprint] Refactor GoogleSecretary to follow Registered Dispatcher pattern
body.description:
GoogleSecretary (T-google-secretary-device) currently has bespoke routing logic for calendar/email/tasks operations. The Registered Dispatcher pattern (C-registered-dispatcher, §3.10 of TheoryOfOperation.md) is now the rack's canonical shape. Refactor GoogleSecretary to use it: capabilities (CalendarHandler, EmailHandler, TasksHandler) self-register at startup; the device dispatcher matches incoming requests to registered handlers by capability tag rather than by explicit conditionals.

This is gated on T-google-secretary-device because the device must exist before it can be refactored.

**Affected files:** devices/google_secretary/ (all — doesn't exist yet; shaped correctly from the start)
**Design rules:** Registered Dispatcher (C-registered-dispatcher); orchestration over monolith (feedback memory); external state principle
**Scope boundary:** In scope: shape the GoogleSecretary device around the three-stack pattern; each capability handler self-registers with its handled request types. Out of scope: implementing the actual Google API integrations — those live in T-google-secretary-device.
**Completion criteria:** GoogleSecretary capability handlers self-register via a register_handler(handles=[...]) call; no hardcoded capability→handler mapping in the dispatcher; existing tests pass; a new handler can be added by calling register_handler() without touching dispatcher code
